elements.algorithm-2411-002: \caption{Directed Weighted DSO with fast query: $\tilde{O}(n^{3/2})$ preprocessing and $\tilde{O}(D)$ query} \begin{algorithmic}[1] \Require Graph $G=(V,E)$. \Ensure Answer queries for replacement path distance $d(x,y,e)$ for each $x,y \in V, e \in E$. \lineComment{Preprocessing Algorithm} \State Compute APSP on $G$ and the reversed graph $G^r$, remembering parent nodes. \State Perform excluded shortest path computations from each vertex $x \in V$, with sets of edges $R_x^{(1)},\cdots, R_x^{(\sqrt{n})}$ where $R_x^{(i)}$ is the set of edges $i$ hops from $x$ in the (out-)shortest path tree $T_x$ rooted at $x$. Repeat in $G^r$. \label{alg:dsoquery:hopexclude} \For{$x \in V$} \lineComment{Tree cutting procedure on $T_x$} \State Perform an upcast along $T_x$, computing size and depth of each vertex, and identify level vertices of both types. Perform an additional upcast and downcast, to identify all type 1 level vertices. (details described in Section~\ref{sec:treecutting}) \label{alg:dsoquery:treecutting} \State Perform a downcast along $T_x$ so that each edge $e \in T_x$ knows the closest level vertex (if there is one) on the path from $x$ to $e$. Repeat in $G^r$. Each edge identifies the subpath between interval vertices it belongs to. \label{alg:dsoquery:cutcast} \State Perform exclude computations from $x$ with the $O(\sqrt{n})$ sets of independent paths identified in line~\ref{alg:dsoquery:cutcast}. \label{alg:dsoquery:treeexclude} \EndFor \lineComment{Query Algorithm: Given query $d(x,y,e)$} \lineComment{$e$ is within $h$ hops from $x$ or $y$ in $T_x$: use excluded edge distances from line~\ref{alg:dsoquery:hopexclude}} \State If $d(x,y,e)$ has been computed in line~\ref{alg:dsoquery:hopexclude} at $x$ or $y$, broadcast $d(x,y,e)$ as the answer. \label{alg:dsoquery:shortans} \lineComment{$e$ is not within $h$ hops from $x$ or $y$ in $T_x$: use excluded interval distances from line~\ref{alg:dsoquery:treeexclude}} \State From line~\ref{alg:dsoquery:cutcast}, $e$ locally determines $a$ as the closest level vertex on the path from $x$ to $e$, and $b$ as the closest level vertex on the path from $e$ to $y$. $e$ broadcasts the identities of $a$ and $b$. \label{alg:dsoquery:querylevel} \State Then, $a$ broadcasts $d(a,y,e)$ and $x$ broadcasts $d(x,a),d(x,b,e)$. Distances $d(a,y,e)$ and $d(x,b,e)$ were computed in line~\ref{alg:dsoquery:hopexclude}. \label{alg:dsoquery:querybroadcast} \State Finally, $y$ broadcasts $d(x,y,e) = \min(d(x,a)+d(a,y,e), d(x,b,e)+d(b,y), d(x,y,[a,b]))$ as the answer. Distance $d(x,y,[a,b])$ was computed in line~\ref{alg:dsoquery:treeexclude}. \label{alg:dsoquery:longans} \end{algorithmic} \label{alg:dsoquery}

elements.algorithm-2411-003: \caption{Directed Weighted DSO with fast preprocessing: $\tilde{O}(n)$ preprocessing and $\tilde{O}(\sqrt{n}+D)$ query} \begin{algorithmic}[1] \Require Graph $G=(V,E)$. \Ensure Answer query for replacement path distance $d(x,y,e)$ for each $x,y \in V, e \in E$. \lineComment{Preprocessing Algorithm} \For{$j=\log (\sqrt{n}), \cdots, \log n$} \label{alg:dsopre:prepst} \lineComment{Preprocessing for replacement paths of hop-length $[2^j,2^{j+1}]$, where $2^j \ge \sqrt{n}$} \State Each vertex $s \in V$ is sampled and added to $S_j$ with probability $\frac{c \log n}{2^j}$. \label{alg:dsopre:samp} \State Let $h=2^{j+1}$. Sample $\tilde{O}(h)$ graphs $G^j_i$ as in Lemma~\ref{lem:sampgraph} by randomly sampling each edge with probability $1-\frac{1}{h}$. \label{alg:dsopre:sampgraph} \rightComment{Each edge locally samples itself} \State For each $e \in G$, $I_e^j \gets \{(j,i) \mid e \not\in G^j_i\}$ \label{alg:dsopre:edgesets} \rightComment{Computed locally at endpoints of $e$} \State Compute SSSP from each vertex in $S_j$, on each $G^j_i$ (and reversed graph). The distance in graph $G^j_i$ is denoted $d^j_i$. Distances $d^j_i(s,x), d^j_i(x,s)$ are computed at $x$ for each $s \in S_j,x \in V$. \label{alg:dsopre:sssp} \EndFor \lineComment{Query Algorithm: Given query $d(x,y,e)$} \lineComment{Find best replacement path of hop-length $\le \sqrt{n}$} \State Perform a distributed Bellman-Ford computation in $G-\{e\}$ starting from $x$, up to $\sqrt{n}$ hops. This computes $d_{\sqrt{n}}(x,y,e)$ at $y$. \label{alg:dsopre:bf} \lineComment{Find best replacement path of hop-length $> \sqrt{n}$} \For{$j=\log (\sqrt{n}), \cdots, \log n$} \lineComment{Handle paths of hop-length $[2^j,2^{j+1}]$} \State Endpoint of $e$ broadcasts $I^j_e$. \label{alg:dsopre:edgesetbroad} \State $x$ broadcasts $\{d^j_i(x,s) \mid (j,i) \in I^j_e, s \in S_j\}$, i.e. its distance to sampled vertices in $S_j$ in graphs where $e$ is not present. \label{alg:dsopre:querybroadcast} \For{$s \in S_j$} \rightComment{Local computation at $y$} \lineComment{$d_s$ is the best $x$-$y$ replacement path distance through $s$} \State Compute $d_s = \min\limits_{(j,i) \in I_e^j} d^j_i(x,s) + d^j_i(s,y)$ \label{alg:dsopre:querysamp} \EndFor \EndFor \State Compute $d(x,y,e) = \min(d_{\sqrt{n}}(x,y,e), \min\limits_{s \in V} d_s$) \rightComment{Local computation at $y$} \label{alg:dsopre:querymin} \end{algorithmic} \label{alg:dsopre}

elements.algorithm-2411-004: \caption{Directed Weighted 2-APSiSP} \begin{algorithmic}[1] \Require Graph $G=(V,E)$. \Ensure Compute 2-SiSP distance $d_2(x,y)$ at $y$ for each $x,y \in V$. \State Compute APSP in $G$ and $G^r$, remembering parent nodes. \State For each vertex $x$, find all neighbors $a$ such that edge $(x,a)$ is a shortest path. Perform a downcast on $T_x$, the out-shortest path tree rooted at $x$, so that the identity of $(x,a)$ and $w(x,a)$ is known to vertices in subtree of $T_x$ rooted at $a$. \label{alg:apsisp:downcast} \State Compute excluded shortest paths from $x$ with the set of edges $(x,a)$ excluded. Thus, we compute $d(x,y,(x,a))$ at all $y$, where $(x,a)$ is the first edge of $x$-$y$ shortest path. \label{alg:apsisp:exclude} \For{$y \in V$} \label{alg:apsisp:forst} \rightComment{Local computation at $y$} \State $y$ computes $d_2(z,y) = d(z,y,(z,y))$ for in-edges $(z,y)$ such that $(z,y)$ is a shortest \label{alg:apsisp:hop1} path. \For{up to $n$ iterations} \label{alg:apsisp:for} \State For $z' \in V$ such that $y$ received distance $d(z',y,(z',z))$ (in line~\ref{alg:apsisp:exclude}) and $d_2(z,y)$ has already been computed, compute $d_2(z',y) = \min(d_2(z,y)+w(z',z), d(z',y,(z',z)))$. Note that $w(z',z)$ was sent to $y$ in line~\ref{alg:apsisp:downcast} as edge $(z',z)$ is a shortest path. \EndFor \EndFor \label{alg:apsisp:foren} \end{algorithmic} \label{alg:apsisp}

elements.algorithm-2411-005: \caption{Single Source Excluded Shortest Paths} \begin{algorithmic}[1] \Require Graph $G=(V,E)$, source $x \in V$, independent set of paths $\mathcal{R}_x$. \Ensure Compute distance $d(x,y,P)$ for each $y \in V, P \in \mathcal{R}_x$. \State Compute SSSP from $x$ to compute $d(x,y)$ at $y$ for each $y \in V$.\label{alg:exclude:sssp} \State Using a downcast from $x$ along $T_x$, communicate start vertex of $P \in \mathcal{R}_x$ to each $z \in T_x(P)$. \label{alg:exclude:edgedown} \State Each vertex $y$ communicates $d(x,y)$ to its neighbors $N_{out}(y)$. If $y$ is in a subtree $T_x(P)$ (as identified in line~\ref{alg:exclude:edgedown}), it communicates the start vertex of $P$ as well to its neighbors. \label{alg:exclude:neighb} \State Each vertex $z \in T_x(P)$ locally computes $d^*(x,z) = \min\limits_{v \in N_{in}(z), v \not\in T_x(P)} (d(x,z)+w(z,y))$. The check for $v \not\in T_x(P)$ is done locally using the information from line~\ref{alg:exclude:neighb}. \label{alg:exclude:edgewt} \State Perform SSSP from $x$ on $G$ with edges in $\mathcal{R}_x$ removed, and added $x$-$z$ edge with weight $d^*(x,z)$. These added edges are simulated locally at $z$ in the course of the SSSP algorithm. \label{alg:exclude:finalsssp} \end{algorithmic} \label{alg:exclude}
elements.sent-2411-482: Each vertex $y$ in $T_x(P)$ sends distance $d(x,y)$ and the start vertex of $P$ to all its neighbors in line~\ref{alg:exclude:neighb .}
elements.algorithm-2411-006: \caption{DSO with fast query: $\tilde{O}(n^{3/2})$ preprocessing and $\tilde{O}(D)$ query} \begin{algorithmic}[1] \Require Graph $G=(V,E)$. \Ensure Answer query for replacement path distance $d(x,y,e)$ for any $x,y \in V, e \in E$. \lineComment{Preprocessing Algorithm} \State Compute APSP on $G$ and the reversed graph $G^r$, remembering parent nodes. \State Perform excluded shortest path computations from each vertex $x \in V$, with sets of edges $R_x^{(1)},\cdots, R_x^{(\sqrt{n})}$ where $R_x^{(i)}$ is the set of edges $i$ hops from $x$ in the out-shortest path tree $T_x$ rooted at $x$. Repeat in $G^r$. \label{alg:dsoquery:hopexclude} \For{$x \in V$} \lineComment{Tree cutting procedure on $T_x$} \State Perform an upcast along $T_x$, computing size and depth of each vertex, and identify level vertices of both types. Perform an additional upcast and downcast, to identify all type 1 level vertices. (details described in Section~\ref{sec:treecutting}) \label{alg:dsoquery:treecutting} \State Perform a downcast along $T_x$ so that each edge $e \in T_x$ knows the closest level vertex (if there is one) on the path from $x$ to $e$. Repeat in $G^r$. Each edge identifies the subpath between interval vertices it belongs to. \label{alg:dsoquery:cutcast} \State Perform exclude computations from $x$ with the $O(\sqrt{n})$ sets of independent paths identified in line~\ref{alg:dsoquery:cutcast}. \label{alg:dsoquery:treeexclude} \EndFor \lineComment{Query Algorithm: Given query $d(x,y,e)$} \lineComment{$e$ is within $h$ hops from $x$ or $y$ in $T_x$: use excluded distances from line~\ref{alg:dsoquery:hopexclude}} \State If $d(x,y,e)$ has been computed in line~\ref{alg:dsoquery:hopexclude} at $x$ or $y$, broadcast $d(x,y,e)$. \label{alg:dsoquery:shortans} \lineComment{$e$ is not within $h$ hops from $x$ or $y$ in $T_x$: use excluded distances from line~\ref{alg:dsoquery:treeexclude}} \State From line~\ref{alg:dsoquery:cutcast}, $e$ locally determines $a$ as the closest level vertex on the path from $x$ to $e$, and $b$ as the closest level vertex on the path from $e$ to $y$. $e$ broadcasts the identities of $a$ and $b$. \label{alg:dsoquery:querylevel} \State Then, $a$ broadcasts $d(a,y,e)$ and $x$ broadcasts $d(x,a),d(x,b,e)$. Distances $d(a,y,e)$ and $d(x,b,e)$ were computed in line~\ref{alg:dsoquery:hopexclude}. \label{alg:dsoquery:querybroadcast} \State Finally, $y$ broadcasts $d(x,y,e) = \min(d(x,a)+d(a,y,e), d(x,b,e)+d(b,y) , d(x,y,[a,b]))$. Distance $d(x,y,[a,b])$ was computed in line~\ref{alg:dsoquery:treeexclude}. \label{alg:dsoquery:longans} \end{algorithmic} \label{alg:dsoquery}

elements.algorithm-2411-007: \caption{DSO with fast preprocessing: $\tilde{O}(n)$ preprocessing and $\tilde{O}(\sqrt{n}+D)$ query} \begin{algorithmic}[1] \Require Graph $G=(V,E)$. \Ensure Answer query for replacement path distance $d(x,y,e)$ for any $x,y \in V, e \in E$. \lineComment{Preprocessing Algorithm} \For{$j=\log (\sqrt{n}), \cdots, \log n$} \label{alg:dsopre:prepst} \lineComment{Preprocessing for replacement paths of hop-length $[2^j,2^{j+1}]$, where $2^j \ge \sqrt{n}$} \State Each vertex $s \in V$ is sampled and added to $S_j$ with probability $\frac{c \log n}{2^j}$. \label{alg:dsopre:samp} \State Let $h=2^{j+1}$. Sample $\tilde{O}(h)$ graphs $G^j_i$ as in Lemma~\ref{lem:sampgraph} by randomly sampling each edge with probability $1-(1/h)$. \label{alg:dsopre:sampgraph} \rightComment{Each edge locally samples itself} \State For each $e \in G$, $I_e^j \gets \{(j,i) \mid e \not\in G^j_i\}$ \label{alg:dsopre:edgesets} \rightComment{Computed locally at endpoints of $e$} \State Compute SSSP from each vertex in $S_j$, on each $G^j_i$ (and reversed graph). The distance in graph $G^j_i$ is denoted $d^j_i$. Distances $d^j_i(s,x), d^j_i(x,s)$ are computed at $x$ for each $s \in S_j,x \in V$. \label{alg:dsopre:sssp} \EndFor \lineComment{Query Algorithm: Given query $d(x,y,e)$} \lineComment{Find best replacement path of hop-length $\le \sqrt{n}$} \State Perform a distributed Bellman-Ford computation in $G-\{e\}$ starting from $x$, up to $\sqrt{n}$ hops. This computes $d_{\sqrt{n}}(x,y,e)$ at $y$. \label{alg:dsopre:bf} \lineComment{Find best replacement path of hop-length $> \sqrt{n}$} \For{$j=\log (\sqrt{n}), \cdots, \log n$} \lineComment{Handle paths of hop-length $[2^j,2^{j+1}]$} \State Endpoint of $e$ broadcasts $I^j_e$. \label{alg:dsopre:edgesetbroad} \State $x$ broadcasts $\{d^j_i(x,s) \mid (j,i) \in I^j_e, s \in S_j\}$, i.e. its distance to sampled vertices in $S_j$ in graphs where $e$ is not present. \label{alg:dsopre:querybroadcast} \For{$s \in S_j$} \rightComment{Local computation at $y$} \lineComment{$d_s$ is the best $x$-$y$ replacement path distance through $s$} \State Compute $d_s = \min\limits_{(j,i) \in I_e^j} d^j_i(x,s) + d^j_i(s,y)$ \label{alg:dsopre:querysamp} \EndFor \EndFor \State Compute $d(x,y,e) = \min(d_{\sqrt{n}}(x,y,e), \min\limits_{s \in V} d_s$) \rightComment{Local computation at $y$} \label{alg:dsopre:querymin} \end{algorithmic} \label{alg:dsopre}
